mcqs.question: Which operation adds an element to the rear of a queue?
mcqs.explanation: Enqueue is the operation that adds an element to the rear (back) of a queue. Push and Pop are stack operations.

mcqs.question: Which operation removes an element from the front of a queue?
mcqs.explanation: Dequeue removes and returns the element at the front of the queue, following the FIFO (First In First Out) principle.

mcqs.question: What condition indicates that a linear queue implemented using an array is full?
mcqs.explanation: In a linear queue using an array, the queue is full when the rear pointer reaches the last index (MAX_SIZE - 1), meaning no more elements can be added.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of enqueue and dequeue operations in a queue implemented using an array?

options.text: O(1) for enqueue, O(n) for dequeue
O(n) for enqueue, O(1) for dequeue

mcqs.explanation: Both enqueue and dequeue operations take O(1) constant time since they only involve adding at the rear or removing from the front using index manipulation.

mcqs.question: The Peek or Front operation on a queue:

options.text: Returns the front element without removing it
Adds an element to the front

mcqs.explanation: The Peek (or Front) operation returns the element at the front of the queue without removing it, allowing inspection of the next element to be dequeued.
